text.title: Filesystem Backup with Restic Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the backup-filesystem-restic methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale and is referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Restic scripts MUST load `RESTIC_PASSWORD` via `RESTIC_PASSWORD_FILE` (mode 0600) or `RESTIC_PASSWORD_COMMAND` from a secrets manager; never pass on the command line or inline-literal in the script.

rationale.source: Restic docs — Environment Variables, OWASP Secrets Management

rule.rationale: Restic encrypts the repository; a lost or wrong password renders all data unrecoverable. CLI args appear in process listings and shell history.

rule.statement: Every Restic repository MUST run `restic check --read-data-subset=5%` at least weekly with an alert on non-zero exit.
rule.rationale: Pack files corrupt silently over months; detection at restore time is the failure case.

rule.statement: Retention scripts MUST run `restic forget --prune` (not bare `forget`); a `forget` without `--prune` leaves orphaned packs holding full storage.
rule.rationale: forget removes snapshot references but does not garbage-collect packs; storage growth continues unbounded.

rule.statement: Backup commands MUST include `--exclude` patterns for `/proc`, `/sys`, `/dev`, `/tmp`, `node_modules`, `.cache`, `*.sock` to avoid wasted backup bandwidth and corrupt snapshots.
rule.rationale: Volatile or virtual filesystems explode snapshot size and can hang the backup.

rule.statement: Production backup wiring MUST use a systemd timer + service unit (with `OnFailure=` alert hook) rather than crond; cron silently swallows failures and lacks structured logging.
rule.rationale: Cron sends mail-only failure notifications that are ignored at scale; systemd integrates with journald + alerting.

rule.statement: If the host is a managed VM (RDS, GKE node, Lambda) with platform-managed snapshots covering the RPO target, or if it carries only ephemeral state, this methodology MUST be skipped.
rule.rationale: Adding Restic on top of managed snapshots duplicates cost and introduces drift.
